# Occupancy probe report (M2.5 Tier-1)

**Date:** 2026-06-06 · **Generated by:** `occupancy_probe` (analytical, CUDA-free) · risk **A2**

> ⚠️ **Device specs are PROVISIONAL.** This Tier-1 model runs on the Mac mini M4 Pro (no NVIDIA GPU). The SM count / warps-per-SM / register & shared-mem limits below are *estimates* — confirm them on the RTX 5080 via `deviceQuery` (Tier-2) and re-run. The **conclusion is robust to the exact numbers** (see sensitivity section).

## Inputs

- **Material:** Al FCC (a=4.05 Å), number density ρ = 0.06022 atoms/Å³.
- **Zone width:** 4.00 Å (= R_cut, M0). A TD zone is a slab of this width across the box cross-section.
- **Kernel estimate:** block_size = 128, 40 regs/thread, 0 B shared-mem/block. *(No compiled kernel yet — M3 will refine via `nvcc -Xptxas -v`.)*
- **Saturation target:** a single kernel launch should fill ≥ 50% of the device.

## Device profiles

| Device | SMs | warps/SM | blocks/SM | regs/SM | smem/SM (KB) | confirmed |
|--------|----:|---------:|----------:|--------:|-------------:|:---------:|
| RTX 5080 (Blackwell GB203) — PROVISIONAL | 84 | 48 | 32 | 65536 | 100 | **no** |
| A100 (GA100) — reference, approx | 108 | 64 | 32 | 65536 | 164 | **no** |
| RTX 4090 (Ada AD102) — reference, approx | 128 | 48 | 24 | 65536 | 99 | **no** |

## Per-SM occupancy of the force kernel

Theoretical occupancy from kernel resources vs per-SM limits (independent of problem size).

| Device | active blocks/SM | active warps/SM | occupancy | limiter |
|--------|-----------------:|----------------:|----------:|---------|
| RTX 5080 (Blackwell GB203) — PROVISIONAL | 12 | 48 | 100% | warps |
| A100 (GA100) — reference, approx | 12 | 48 | 75% | registers |
| RTX 4090 (Ada AD102) — reference, approx | 12 | 48 | 100% | warps |

> A high per-SM occupancy here does **not** mean the device is busy: that depends on whether the problem supplies enough warps (next section). This is the crux of A2.

## Device fill vs system size — RTX 5080 (Blackwell GB203) — PROVISIONAL

Single thin zone (width 4.0 Å), per-SM occupancy 100% → device holds 4032 warps at once.

| N atoms | box L (Å) | zones in box | atoms/zone | warps/zone | single-zone fill | zones to saturate |
|--------:|----------:|-------------:|-----------:|-----------:|-----------------:|------------------:|
| 72 | 11 | 2 | 27 | 1 | 0.0% | 4032 |
| 1000 | 26 | 6 | 157 | 5 | 0.1% | 807 |
| 10000 | 55 | 13 | 728 | 23 | 0.6% | 176 |
| 100000 | 118 | 29 | 3378 | 106 | 2.6% | 39 |
| 1000000 | 255 | 63 | 15679 | 490 | 12.2% | 9 |
| 10000000 | 550 | 137 | 72774 | 2275 | 56.4% | 2 |

## Sensitivity to device specs (N = 1000000)

The single-zone-underfill conclusion holds across plausible profiles, so it does not hinge on the provisional 5080 numbers.

| Device | device warp cap | warps/zone | single-zone fill | zones to saturate |
|--------|----------------:|-----------:|-----------------:|------------------:|
| RTX 5080 (Blackwell GB203) — PROVISIONAL | 4032 | 490 | 12.2% | 9 |
| A100 (GA100) — reference, approx | 5184 | 490 | 9.5% | 11 |
| RTX 4090 (Ada AD102) — reference, approx | 6144 | 490 | 8.0% | 13 |

## Verdict (go / no-go)

- 🔴 **A single thin zone underutilizes the GPU.** At N=1000000 a 4.0 Å zone fills only **12.2%** of RTX 5080 (Blackwell GB203) — PROVISIONAL (per-SM occupancy is 100% — the gap is purely *too few warps in flight*).
- 🟢 **Batching recovers it.** Running **≈9 zones concurrently** per kernel/stream reaches the ≥50% target (63 zones available in the box → feasible).
- The tiny golden system (N=72) is a **force-correctness fixture, not a performance target** — a single zone there is ~1 warp.
- Small/medium systems (≤~10⁶ atoms) underfill on one zone; very large systems (≥~10⁷) approach saturation on a single zone.

### Recommendation for M3

1. The clustering/conveyor **must process multiple zones concurrently** (batch ≈ 9 for the 5080 class) — a one-zone-at-a-time kernel will leave the GPU idle on typical sizes.
2. Keep the kernel register-light (it hit 100% per-SM occupancy at 40 regs/thread, block 128).
3. Treat zone width as an occupancy tuning knob (≥ R_cut), not just a causality parameter.

## Reproduce / confirm on target

```bash
# Tier-1 (any host, no GPU):
./build/occupancy_probe > docs/_meta/occupancy_probe_2026-06-06.md

# On the RTX 5080, get real specs and feed them back:
#   deviceQuery   # multiProcessorCount, maxThreadsPerMultiProcessor, ...
./build/occupancy_probe --sm <SMs> --warps-per-sm <W> \
    --regs <regs/thread> --block <block> --confirmed "RTX 5080"
```

**Tier-2 (deferred to GPU):** validate this prediction with `cudaOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessor` and Nsight Compute `sm__warps_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active` on the real force kernel (see Roadmap M2.5 / M3).
